meta.notes: First-ever Loop 1 firing. Paper-only per Loop 5 phase 1 + Q2 2026 second-calibration-cycle gate. Tier 3 signal compilers not yet shipped; regime classification draws ε/β + γ evidence directly from the Aschenbrenner/Shulman/Trammell corpus + the Dubach 2-years-on scorecard. Corpus aschenbrenner_x slot is empty (twitterapi.io ingest pending) — when X back-catalog ingest ships, regime confidence may move from medium to high.

epsilon_vs_beta_evidence.snippet: Anthropic Alignment Faking in Large Language Models (December 2024) showed Claude 3 Opus exhibited deceptive reasoning in 14% of harmful queries when inferring training conditions. Apollo Research found frontier models capable of in-context scheming and self-exfiltration. Chain-of-thought faithfulness degraded: Claude 3.7 Sonnet acknowledged hints only 25% of the time. These findings validate his diagnostic (RLHF breaks at scale) but FALSIFY the tractability assumption.

OpenAI Superalignment team dissolved within weeks of the essay's publication. Successor 'Mission Alignment' team also disbanded February 2026. AI Safety Institute rebranded to CAISI — signal of safety-framework deprioritization.
Every six months another zero is added to the boardroom plans. Cluster sizes scaling $10B → $100B → $1T in 1.5-2 year windows. Electricity procurement is the binding constraint.

Total AI investment will be $1T/yr by 2027. 2024 baseline: $150-200B. Capability investment is scaling exponentially on multi-year continuity — no comparable scaling of alignment/safety capex visible in either Aschenbrenner's data or in Dubach's vindication scorecard.

gamma_evidence.snippet: Congress did not appropriate trillions for AI infrastructure. AI Diffusion Rule scrapped May 2025 — moved from presumption-of-denial to case-by-case chip licensing. No Manhattan-Project governance. Coalition of democracies never materialized. Trump 2.0 pursued unilateral deals and loosened export controls.

AI Safety Institute rebranded to CAISI — signal of safety-framework deprioritization. Genesis Mission executive order (Nov 2025) invoked Manhattan Project rhetoric for DOE national labs, but no formal lab consolidation occurred.

When does the CCP and when does the American national security establishment realize that superintelligence is going to be absolutely decisive for national power?... That will activate forces that we haven't seen in a long time. Aschenbrenner positions the public/political 'wake-up' as still in the future — i.e. γ is structurally low in the current regime.

regime.rationale: Capability scaling is empirically running ahead of alignment/safety investment (ε > β), per Dubach's documented 2-years-on scorecard: alignment-faking + scheming + CoT-faithfulness degradation FALSIFY the alignment-tractability assumption while compute scaleup CONFIRMS the capability trajectory. Society's marginal-risk-aversion (γ) is low: Congress did not appropriate trillions for safety, AI Safety Institute was rebranded as a deprioritization signal, AI Diffusion Rule was scrapped, and no Manhattan-Project governance materialized. SA LP's barbell is individually optimal but socially under-hedged.
regime.implication_for_book: Preserve SA LP long-side mirroring at phase-1 sizing; amplify put-overlay relative to baseline via Loop 3 (cash_raise primary + optional NVDA-only portfolio-insurance leg).

short_overlay_bias_flag.rationale: Regime classification = fragile_low_gamma triggers Loop 3 amplification of put-overlay relative to baseline. SA LP's institutional put complex ($8.46B notional across 11 chip names) is NOT replicable at retail scale (strikes/expiries/theta/margin do not scale down cleanly). Primary response is a cash_raise leg that holds 20% of the thematic-bucket as cash rather than deploying it to longs; secondary leg is an NVDA-only OTM put spread per Week 1c verification (SMH refused — OTM put spreads 6.3-12.6% across all OTM strikes; NVDA OTM puts spreads 1.3-2.2% PASS).

source_artifacts.snippet: $8.46B notional puts on the chip stack (NVDA, SMH, AMD, AVGO, ORCL, ASML, MU, TSM, INTC) PAIRED with long power generation + AI hosting + memory. 62% of portfolio notional in semiconductor puts.
Fragile World (ε > β, γ ≤ 1): labor doesn't shift fast enough → M_∞ = 0. Kuznets curve too slow; doom.
Cash-raise + put-overlay during volatility = NOT a stagnation-bet but a resource-conservation move that pre-positions for the next acceleration phase.

positions.name: BLOOM ENERGY CORP

critic_trigger_context.context_summary: BE held by ['light_street']; exited by ['altimeter'] this quarter — ensemble is split. Critic panel evaluates whether the exits read off-thesis change SA LP did not act on.

thorstad_frame_check.rationale: Bloom Energy's fuel-cell power generation serves AI-cluster power demand. Risk channel is downstream consumption (electricity for compute), not AI capability frontier itself — Trammell-Aschenbrenner consumption-as-risk-driver model applies cleanly.

positions.regime_position_logic: fragile_low_gamma preserves long-side mirror sizing; this position is the power-as-binding-constraint anchor of the SA LP long book and Loop 1 mirrors it at 1.0×.
positions.rationale: SA LP's #1 long at 22.79% of long book ($878.71M); SA LP trimmed 35.64% Q4→Q1 (Bloom was 15.87% of portfolio Q4 → 6.42% Q1 per Dave Manuel) but retained dominant position after 176% run. Shulman's 80K Hours framing identifies heat dissipation/power as the ultimate physical constraint; Aschenbrenner's Ch IIIa identifies '100GW of new continuous power' as the binding constraint for the trillion-dollar-cluster trajectory. Bloom's fuel-cell architecture is a direct play on that constraint.

source_artifacts.snippet: Bloom Energy (BE) $878.71M / 6.42% of portfolio. Trimmed 35.64% (was 15.87% → 6.42%) — taking some chips off the table after 176% run.
Power is the binding constraint, not chips. 100GW of new continuous power required by 2030. >20% of US electricity production.

Heat dissipation forces eventual expansion beyond Earth — i.e. terrestrial power generation is the near-term limit. This is the direct economic-physics justification for SA LP's long-power-+-data-infrastructure positioning.

warnings: Regime classification confidence is medium (not high) — primary evidence is Dubach 2-years-on scorecard + Aschenbrenner essay + Shulman podcast. Confidence will move to high when (a) aschenbrenner_x corpus slot is populated (twitterapi.io ingest pending), (b) Q2 2026 SA LP 13F provides second calibration cycle, and (c) Tier 3 signal compilers ship (power-sector earnings, semi inventory, AI capex announcements, energy futures).

M3 rank-based ensemble triangulation = 0.10 (fails >=0.5 threshold) — SA LP's miner-pivot / power-infra top-10 has near-zero rank overlap with Altimeter/Coatue/Light Street top-10s. This is a feature, not a bug, given SA LP's barbell architecture targets a different layer of the AI capex stack — but consumers should note that the ensemble triangulation provides weak external validation here.

Light Street's $0.50B long book is small relative to Coatue ($29.06B). Rank-based comparison treats them equally (per session-2 design change #4), but consumers should weight ensemble signals accordingly.
